steps.title: Identify the passage's structure and focus
steps.content: The passage opens by naming Fannie Barrier Williams and calling her instrumental, then immediately gives a concrete example: she secured job placements for Black women before they even left the South. This tell-then-show structure signals the author's main purpose is to introduce and illustrate her role.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — To introduce and illustrate Barrier Williams's integral role in supporting other Black women as their circumstances changed during part of the Great Migration: Matches the passage exactly — it names her, calls her instrumental, then illustrates her work securing jobs. ✓
  • Choice B — To establish that Barrier Williams used her professional connections to arrange employment for other Black women, including jobs with the Chicago Defender: Adds an unsupported detail — the passage never says she used connections from the Chicago Defender to find jobs. ✗
  • Choice C — To demonstrate that the factors that motivated the start of the Great Migration were different for Black women than they were for Black men: Confuses supporting context with main purpose — the gender difference in employment opportunities is background, not the author's primary claim. ✗
  • Choice D — To provide an overview of the employment challenges faced by Black women in the agricultural and domestic spheres in the southern United States: Treats a supporting detail as the main idea — employment challenges are mentioned only to explain why Barrier Williams's help mattered. ✗
That is the correct answer.
